openQuestions.question: What exact top-1/margin deltas appear after deleting deep-loop code-audit metadata and reindexing?
openQuestions.nextEvidence: Run advisor rebuild and 193-row scorer validation in a write-authorized implementation branch.

openQuestions.question: Which command metadata files should be authoritative for derived command bridges?
openQuestions.nextEvidence: Inventory command docs/registries and produce bridge candidates before replacing COMMAND_BRIDGES.

resolvedQuestions.answer: Single-pass code audit vocabulary should be owned by sk-code/code-review; deep-loop-workflows should own loop/iteration/convergence review vocabulary only.
Metadata should own hub/mode aliases; scorer constants should keep global syntax, slash-command, and post-fusion disambiguators until measurement proves them redundant.
Do not change semantic_shadow weight in this workstream; pursue mcp-* description/embedding hygiene instead.

keyFindings.summary: Parent-hub code-audit vocabulary is still projected from deep-loop-workflows and forces a scorer workaround.
The existing vocab sync guard is single-hub; cross-hub normalized phrase collisions need an owner map by hub and intent class.
Projection guard coverage should validate intentSignals, derivedTriggers, and derivedKeywords as consumed by the scorer.
Hardcoded command bridges are a second authority; executor delegation provides a metadata-derived alias-table precedent.
Semantic-shadow false-fires point to mcp-* description hygiene, not lane-weight changes.

ruledOutDirections: Production scorer edits in this lineage
Deleting codeAuditDeepReviewPenalty before metadata correction and rebaseline
Global collision failure for every shared infrastructure phrase
Semantic-shadow lane-weight churn without a separate measured PROVE branch
Broad conflicts_with authoring for all sibling skills

promotedIdeas.idea: Workspace-level parent-hub collision report mapping normalized phrase -> hub -> mode/intent class -> projected fields.
Validate registry aliases against actual SkillProjection fields, not only graph-metadata trigger phrases.
